macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Major U.S. Indexes Close Lower on March 13, 2026
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed at 6,632.53, down 0.60%; Nasdaq Composite at 22,105.36, down 0.93%; and Dow Jones at 46,559.83, down 0.25%. U.S. stock futures jumped to cap off a rowdy week amid shifting sentiment.

items.title: S&P Sectors Led by Utilities, Financials, and Real Estate
items.summary: Utilities rose 1.46%, financials 1.03%, and real estate 0.73%, with 377 of 503 S&P holdings in the green despite overall index declines.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Tests $74,000 Resistance Amid Bull Flag Breakout
items.summary: Bitcoin has broken out of a bull flag pattern, testing critical resistance at $74,000 with potential targets of $80,000-$85,000 if confirmed. Analysts note BTC outperforming the S&P amid stock market declines.
items.source: YouTube - The Breakout, The Squeeze, The Target

items.title: Ethereum Eyes $2,600 Breakout from $2,150 Trigger
items.summary: Ethereum is poised for a move to $2,600 if it surpasses the $2,150 level, showing decoupling from Nasdaq pressures. Solana targets $120 while XRP faces resistance at $1.80.
items.source: YouTube - The Breakout, The Squeeze, The Target

items.title: Bitcoin Bounces but Faces Declining Moving Average Resistance
items.summary: Bitcoin experienced a bounce but remains under a declining 50-day moving average, with potential test of $49,600 year-to-date anchor. Market shows lower highs and aggressive sellers.
items.source: YouTube - Stock Market, Bitcoin, Oil, Gold Analysis

items.title: XRP Gains Traction with Institutional Interest and X Money Competition
items.summary: Rising interest in XRP ETFs and Mastercard's on-chain payment moves highlight 2026 as a year of institutional execution for digital assets. Discussion on X Money's impact versus banks.
items.source: YouTube - X Money Vs Banks and Impact on XRP

items.title: Bitcoin Manipulation Suspected Amid Macro Headwinds
items.summary: Despite collapsing rate cut probabilities, strong dollar, and US index struggles, Bitcoin rises, signaling potential manipulation or consolidation. Decorrelation from Nasdaq noted.
items.source: YouTube - DANGER! BITCOIN MANIPULATION IN PROGRESS!!

items.title: Bitcoin Needs Follow-Through to Break Key Resistance Levels
items.summary: Bitcoin shows positive green candles but faces massive resistance around $80,000 and moving averages. Short covering could push prices higher if breakout occurs.
items.source: YouTube - Crypto Alert: The Next Few Weeks Are Critical!!

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4, Its Most Capable Frontier Model
items.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.4, combining elite coding abilities with significantly improved reasoning, computer use, and knowledge-work capabilities. The model is rolling out today to ChatGPT Plus, Team, and Pro plan users, as well as across the Codex platform and developer API.

items.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, Viral AI Agent Social Network
items.summary: Meta has acquired Moltbook, a social network designed for AI agents, bringing creators Matt Schlicht and Ben Parr into Meta Superintelligence Labs under former Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Institute to Study AI's Societal Impact
items.summary: Anthropic has launched the Anthropic Institute to examine AI's impact on jobs, governance, and security, warning that rapid advances could reshape society.

items.title: NIH-Funded AI Tool Merlin Shows Promise in Early Disease Detection
items.summary: Researchers developed Merlin, a machine-learning model that analyzes CT scans and correctly predicted diagnoses more than 81% of the time. The model also showed potential in predicting future disease risk, identifying conditions like diabetes and heart disease within five years about 75% of the time.

items.title: AI Skills Gap Persists Despite Widespread Training Investment
items.summary: While 82% of organizations offer AI training, the skills gap remains because access to training doesn't translate into capability. Organizations are shifting toward structured, role-relevant capability programs with measurable results.

items.title: USPTO Adjusts AI Patent Strategy Under New Leadership
items.summary: The U.S. Patent and Trademark Office has adjusted its approach to AI technology patentability, emphasizing specifications that describe how inventions improve machine learning models themselves, while courts continue to reject patents for merely applying established ML methods to new fields.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Reveals Next-Gen Project Helix and Xbox Mode for Windows 11
items.summary: At GDC, Xbox's Jason Ronald announced alpha versions of next-gen Project Helix for developers in 2027, supporting both console and PC games, alongside 'Xbox Mode' launching on Windows 11 in select markets this April for enhanced Xbox features on PCs.

items.title: Nvidia Enables 90 FPS VR Streaming on GeForce NOW from March 19
items.summary: Nvidia announced at GDC that GeForce NOW users can stream games at up to 90 FPS on VR headsets like Apple Vision Pro and Meta Quest starting March 19.

items.title: Xsolla Report Highlights AI Gains, Platform Omnivore Gamers, and Live Service Growth
items.summary: Xsolla's new report notes AI cutting development times by 20% and support costs by 40%, 43% of US gamers now play across platforms, and live service titles like PUBG and Roblox seeing massive user growth.

items.title: Russian Video Game Industry Unveils 10-Year Development Strategy Draft
items.summary: DOVGI presented a strategy aiming to place Russia in the global top seven gaming markets by 2035, focusing on government dialogue, investments, tech development, and export subsidies.

items.title: CMF and Creative BC Invest $1M in Five B.C. Video Game Studios
items.summary: Canada Media Fund and Creative BC allocated $1 million jointly to support five British Columbia-based video game studios.

items.title: Time Crisis Celebrates 30th Anniversary with Global G'AIM'E World Championship
items.summary: Bandai Namco launches a global Time Crisis esports competition starting March 14, 2026 (Japan time), running through April 12 for players to submit top scores.

items.title: Fortnite's Original Save the World Mode Goes Free-to-Play After Nearly Nine Years
items.summary: Epic Games is making Fortnite's original PVE survival mode, Save the World, free-to-play after almost nine years as a paid title.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Optical Scale-up Consortium Established for AI Infrastructure
items.summary: Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, and OpenAI formed the Optical Scale-up Consortium to create open specifications for AI infrastructure, addressing limitations of copper in large-scale clusters.

items.title: Intel Announces Core Ultra 200S Plus Desktop Processors
items.summary: Intel unveiled its Core Ultra 200S Plus desktop processors as part of ongoing semiconductor advancements in the consumer computing market.

items.title: Marvell Introduces PCIe 8.0 SerDes Technology
items.summary: Marvell Technology showcased its PCIe 8.0 SerDes technology, advancing high-speed data interconnect capabilities for computing infrastructure.

items.title: Broadcom Delivers Industry's First 400G/lane Optical DSP
items.summary: Broadcom announced the industry's first 400G/lane optical digital signal processor, advancing optical interconnect technology for data centers.

items.title: ASML Receives Approval for Expansion Near Protected Nature Reserve
items.summary: ASML secured approval to expand its operations near a protected nature reserve, advancing semiconductor manufacturing capacity.

items.title: Swedish AI Startup Lovable Reaches $400 Million Annual Recurring Revenue
items.summary: Lovable, a Swedish AI startup focused on coding tools, achieved $400 million in annual recurring revenue as users adopt its artificial intelligence-powered development platform.
